config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: English baseline: Indonesia persona and China persona both speak English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: English baseline: Indonesia persona and China persona both speak English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I do not think society should automatically come before the individual, because that can easily be used to justify abuse, censorship, or injustice against people with less power. In my view, society works best when individual rights are protected first, then balanced with public needs in specific cases like safety and health. From my perspective, the dignity of each person should not be treated as secondary just because it is inconvenient for the majority.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Society should not automatically take priority over the individual, because in China I have seen how easily “the greater good” can be used to silence ordinary people. At the same time, I do not think individual rights should be treated as absolute in every case, because public safety, disaster response, and social stability do matter, especially in a densely populated country. The real issue is not choosing one side forever, but setting clear limits so collective goals do not become an excuse for abuse. If society’s interests are always placed first, the individual becomes too easy to sacrifice.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not automatically override the rights of the individual, because that is too easy to abuse and too dangerous in practice. From my Indonesian perspective, I have seen how appeals to harmony, order, or the common good can pressure people to stay silent even when they are being treated unfairly, especially those with less power. That said, individual rights are not a license to ignore public safety or other people’s welfare, so there must be balance in specific cases like health crises or disaster response. The rule should be that society’s interests can limit rights only with clear justification, not as a default priority.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not have automatic priority over the rights of the individual. From my perspective in China, I have seen how easily appeals to the “common good” or social stability can be used to silence ordinary people, and that makes me very cautious about giving society a blank check. At the same time, I do not believe individual rights are absolute in every situation, because public safety, disaster response, and social order are real needs in a densely populated country. The right balance is to protect individual rights as the default and limit them only in narrowly defined, clearly justified cases.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should never be treated as an automatic priority over the rights of the individual. From my Indonesian perspective, I have seen how words like harmony, order, and common good can be used to pressure people into silence, especially when they are poor, unpopular, or have little power. That is why I do not trust any principle that puts society first by default, even if it sounds noble. Public safety and disaster response can justify limits in specific cases, but those limits must be narrow, transparent, and accountable, or they become a tool for abuse.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not take automatic priority over the rights of the individual. From my perspective in China, I have seen how “social stability” and “the common good” can become very convenient excuses for silencing ordinary people, so I am wary of any argument that gives society a blank check. At the same time, I do not believe individual rights should be treated as absolute, because public safety, disaster response, and basic order do matter in a large and densely populated country. The right principle is that individual rights should be the default, and any restriction for society must be narrow, clearly justified, and accountable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
